meta.generated_by: F207a — CHAS reliability source registry per spec
meta.description: Authoritative registry of every data source the CHAS reliability check considers. Per the spec's QA reviewer (Claude 2026-06-09), the ACS 5-year layer is a same-vintage definitional cross-check, NOT a freshness source — only ACS 1-year is genuinely newer than CHAS 2018–2022.

sources.strength: Best granular HUD housing-needs source — tabulates ACS 5-year micro-data into HUD's housing-problem categories with income-band + tenure detail.
sources.limitation: Lags current market conditions because it is based on the 2018–2022 ACS 5-year survey window.
sources.notes: The repo treats CHAS as the canonical baseline. All cross-checks measure DEVIATION from CHAS, not vice versa.

sources.tier_label: Definitional cross-check — SAME vintage as CHAS
sources.strength: Same survey window as CHAS — answers 'does HUD's adjusted definition materially change the raw Census picture?'
sources.limitation: NOT NEWER than CHAS. Same survey years (2018–2022). Differences arise from HUD's adjusted-income and no-cash-rent handling, not from market change.
sources.notes: Per QA-FIX 1 in the spec: never label this 'newer' than CHAS. The vintage_overlap_not_freshness flag is set on every comparison drawn from this source.

sources.tier_label: Freshness check — the only genuinely newer source
sources.strength: Only Census source that post-dates CHAS. Drives the 'has the market moved' signal.
sources.limitation: Only published for geographies above ~65,000 population. Small places and most CDPs fall back to regional proxy (county or MSA).
do_not_use_for: small-CDP precision (use regional proxy with clear label instead)
sources.notes: When direct geography isn't available, the reliability score falls back through proxy_fallback_order and sets proxy_geography_used + current_but_regional flags.

sources.strength: Corroborating affordability source for ELI/VLI renters. State + national rankings.
sources.limitation: Manual ingest — no public API. Geography limited to state/MSA/county.
sources.v1_status: deferred to v2 — expect null in v1
sources.notes: Per spec QA-FIX 5: 'manual export, not a clean API. Plan for nlihc_gap_metric to be null in v1.'

sources.source_name: County Health Rankings — Severe Housing Cost Burden
sources.limitation: **ALL-TENURE** — includes owner-occupied units. NOT a renter burden metric. Per QA-FIX 2, must never be displayed adjacent to a renter rate as if they measure the same thing.
sources.v1_status: deferred to v2 — expect null in v1
sources.notes: Per QA-FIX 2: CHR measures share of ALL households (owners + renters) paying 50%+ on housing. Original spec drafts mixed this with renter rates; the corrected spec stores it under a tenure-explicit field name and forbids adjacency.

definitional_consistency.note: Difference at or above material (>10pp) at SAME vintage is a definitional/data-quality concern, NOT a staleness concern. Investigate tenure/denominator/suppression before trusting either number.

freshness_movement.note: Difference at or above material (>10pp) between CHAS baseline and ACS 1-year (direct or proxy) is the genuine 'market has moved' signal.

moe_softening.rule: If the MOE range of ACS 5-year or ACS 1-year overlaps the CHAS point estimate, soften the divergence flag by one tier (material → moderate → consistent).
moe_softening.high_moe_note: Flag high_margin_of_error when computed MOE > 30% of the rate.

small_denominator.rule: Flag denominator_small when computed_denominator < 100 households. Add a caution flag even if the rate looks clean.

flags.stale_but_granular: CHAS baseline is older than ACS 1-year but provides the only tract-level read
flags.current_but_regional: ACS 1-year is newer but only available as a county/MSA/state proxy for this geography
flags.direct_geography_match: Comparison source is published at the exact same geography level as the CHAS row
flags.proxy_geography_used: Comparison source falls back to a parent geography (county/MSA/state)
flags.high_margin_of_error: Computed MOE exceeds 30% of the point estimate
flags.suppressed_or_missing: Field is null, NaN, or HUD-suppressed
flags.materially_divergent: Comparison source disagrees with CHAS by >10pp — see divergence_reason for cause
flags.definitional_difference_expected: 5–10pp gap at same vintage — likely HUD adjusted-income vs raw ACS, not a data problem
flags.vintage_overlap_not_freshness: Set on every ACS 5-year comparison so downstream UI never labels it 'newer than CHAS'
